reviews.review_description: Terrible game with fundamental design problems that came out with so much hype and immediately flopped, resulting in the Devs cutting their losses and bailing on it after release.

This app has a lot of potential. After 20-30 minutes I'm sweating and my heart is going. Unfortunately, as others have pointed out, there are issues that distract from the enjoyment. While a lot of reviews are noting hit-detection, I'm having a different issue - or the same issue sseen from another point of view. It seems that after some time playing, the tracking seems to become out of sync. ex: When I hit an enemy in the face, it seems to think I'm hitting it in the body.

The other major issue I have with the app is the between-fight traversal. I love the idea of running to keep my heart rate up. Unfortunately, it's almost impossible (for me at least) to try to run in place with the headset on and move the stick to follow the path to collect the orbs. It's very disorienting. I end up turning the running to "auto" and just doing squats. I'd love it if the app gave you a squat option, or, even better, let you run but automatically followed the path.

I rarely write reviews but I really think this app has a lot of potential and, given a little love, could be a 5-star, use everyday, workout app. Hope the devs are listening.

I love the fact that this game gets my blood pumping, an actual proper competitor to the Nintendo Fitness stuff.
But, punching needs some real improvement. The Spore enemies that come and your only defense is to punch the spores, half the time they don't detect any hits, sometimes to the point where it kills my character and ends my run.
Once they get that figured out, I'll probably be a regular at this! One of the few games that gets your whole body going.

I think the concept of the game is great, but it's missing a few things to become a great fitness game.

1. As other user mentioned the hit detection need some work, I feel it has been improved recently, but sometimes it is unclear why the enemy doesn't have hit reaction even thought you felt that you punched in the right spot, maybe the dev could put better visual indication on the spot the player is supposed to hit, also indicate whether a direct, hook or uppercut is expected ?

2. Navigation, I like the idea, however I would like to have a mode where the left thumb stick allow you to strafe rather than turn, I would also love more content and more diversity for this part of the game, I wouldn't mind having to do some hard squat training (am already doing it)

3. Customization, so this is where I feel the game is lacking the most, even thought there is an equipment system right now there is no way to customize your character appearance and that is a shame really. I would want to incorporate the game in a daily routine, but I feel I would get tired of it very fast due to the lack of contents.

I really want more of this kind of game in VR !

reviews.review_title: Love the game lost 2lb just in a month
reviews.review_description: love this game great workout game i would recommend to do everyone i know

Hit detection sucks. Locomotion sucks. Charecter "progression" sucks. Stats delivery sucks. If the calorie counter can even be remotly trusted all its good for is burning calories and getting annoyed.

Really enjoying the gameified exerice in this.

What i really wish would change is the turning feature, right now i turn with the controller, but i wuld love if it turned with my head facing insted. becouse the joystick turn can be a bit wierd at times.

Im giving this a 2 star, since there seem to be nowhere to contact the devs of the game with suggestions or update, they seem to have dropped from the earth since march after the last vr patch

This game is a good workout, but it's incredibly frustrating and immersion breaking with how frequently the game refuses to recognize a hook or an uppercut. On top of that the visual cues on whether an enemy is invulnerable or whether you're blocking are nowhere to be found, leaving you confused and frustrated as to why a hit didn't land or why you took damage. This whole game just feels bad to play.

I really didn’t want to do this. Please leave a thumbs up to warn others. I have had the game since the first release date. When I started to play I got motion sickness really bad but I didn’t want to leave a bad review and potentially hurt the company so I simply reached out to the developers through Discord. They took my input and that was that. Fast forward I saw they offered resistance bands that could be used for any game so I spent $129 to get them with an advertised ship date of Feb 28th. I reach out to them in the 28th and asked if I’d get a tracking number when it was sent and they replied yes. Come the following Tuesday I sent them an email saying I hadn’t heard anything and asked if my purchase had been sent, no response. I then sent another email on Thursday asking the same thing, no response. As of now the website still has these bands for sale saying they are in stock and available for overnight shipping. I still don’t have mine a week after the advertised ship date

Great work on the latest update.

I uninstalled the game after getting halfway through the tutorial when it first came out. The running was a nightmare — felt like I was drifting all over the place. Fighting was fun, but it didn’t really register properly. It just didn’t feel good to play.

This latest update is almost perfect. Running with your arms instead of your legs removes that weird drifting and the slight disorientation when moving side to side. It’s way smoother now. Only thing I’d add is an option to make the track auto-adjust to the gems, so you don’t have to manually turn. That way you can just focus on going fast — arms or legs — and not worry about constantly fiddling with the thumbstick. Even head-based turning would feel better, honestly.

Adding an arena just for fighting is easily the best change. That’s the part I care about most — the fitness fighting. It’s fun, it’s clean, and the different enemy styles make it way more interesting than dodging flying orbs in most VR boxing games.

Would love to see spells added into the arena too, like the hex section in the tutorial. It’d be a great way to practise them while keeping the pace up.

But seriously — well done. You stuck with it, improved it, and now the game’s back in my library. Looking forward to unlocking more of the arena stuff… just gotta slog through the main game first.

reviews.review_title: Rough start i hope they turn it around.
reviews.review_description: I'll give this a generous 4th star because the company are amazing people with integrity who I hope will succeed. They gave copies to everyone who kickstarted their physical exercise gimmick this was made for as well as not dropping the hardware - good people! And then they are going for a flat price instead of a gross subscription! But this VR port of their flatscreen game is *rough*. It makes all the motion sickness mistakes of first generation VR titles or enthusiast ports like Doom or Quake. Fast, jerky forced movement at what seems to be a low refresh rate makes for an incredibly unpleasant environment I don't want to spend time in even for free and with a stomach of iron, so I only expect to boot this up again in a few month's time to see its progress. The workout itself is exceptionally well considered if you can look past that.

I played yesterday for the first time and was concerned that I would get motion sickness with the running parts as it does move fast. I do get motion sickness on some of my other games after 30 minutes of play. On this one I didn’t. I felt great after an hour of play! I I didn’t intend to play that long but i was having so much fun the time just flew by! Today my arms are sore, mission accomplished! I have another workout app I love but this one is awesome with the addition of the resistance bands! I will be alternating the two. I would like the ability to turn without using the joy sticks but all in all this is a great game! Thanks so much for creating this game and helping me achieve my fitness goals!

I really like Shardfall as a run in place and shadowbox app. I need more fun HIIT activities and this one is great!

That said, I have a hard time staying in place AND steering with the left thumbstick to grab the orbs on the path. 

I would like to request two settings options:

1) To be able to steer with my head movement instead of the thumbstick.

2) To be able to focus on running in place without needing to steer anything at all.

I think this will just make the app more accessible to all.

I don't suffer from motion sensation issues, but if you do, I recommend adjusting the vignette settings they have provided.

It's a fun fitness game, reminds quite a bit of RingFit on Nintendo. Core gameplay loop is running on the spot to drive your character forward, then you engage in boxing combat with different enemies. Each run lasts about 20 mins and you unlock more challenging enemies to fight but your character gets upgrades and becomes more powerful (while you get fitter IRL). 

There is an auto run feature so you don't have to run on the spot if you have bad knees and instead could use that time to stretch, or if you really want to challenge yourself do non-stop body weight squats

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

Thank you kindly for your review and recap of Shardfall: FitQuest VR. Please stay tuned for exciting updates in the future!

We'd also love to hear all about your fitness journey and for your feedback on how we can improve the experience. Please join our Discord! https://discord.com/invite/TzCrSpbjAZ

reviews.review_title: Great idea but poor adaptation to VR…
reviews.review_description:
The concept of the game is fantastic, but unfortunately, the VR adaptation falls short. I genuinely hope the developers address these issues because, as it stands, I cannot recommend purchasing the game.

Here are the main problems:

1. Running segments: The running speed feels excessively fast, even when adjusted to the lowest setting in the options. This creates an experience more akin to a racing game with unnatural, smooth turning. The game should allow players to turn using head movements, which would feel more like jogging—enhancing immersion and comfort. The speed needs to be decreased . There is vignette in the options but it doesn’t fix these issues. 

2. Combat mechanics: Combat feels like you’re punching a screen with an enemy behind it. There are frequent awkward moments where punches fail to connect, leaving you punching the air. This significantly detracts from the experience.

3. Forced camera movements: In many situations, the game moves the camera automatically, similar to a flatscreen game. This can lead to motion sickness and breaks immersion.

The game has immense potential, but these issues need to be resolved to deliver an enjoyable VR experience.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to leave a constructive review of Shardfall: FitQuest VR. The whole studio is hugely appreciative of this feedback as we're actively working on updates and patches to improve our game. 

I want to address a few of your points in particular: 

1. Running segments: We're releasing an update next week that will improve the wider traversal experience. We also have plans to continue work on running segments after the udpate goes live based on the feedback we receive from yourself and the wider community. 

2. Combat Mechanics: Thank you for this feedback. It has been a common theme from our users. As such, it's  one of the top priorities for our studio. You can expect improvements after the update is released next week. Over the following few weeks you can also expect a larger patch that should fix this issue altogether. 

3. Forced Camera Movements: Thank you for this feedback as well. The team is working on this issue and we hope to  have a release timeline available soon for this improvement. 

We really appreciate your constructive feedback — please keep an eye out for our incoming updates, and feel free to join us on Discord to share more thoughts with the team: https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM.

reviews.review_description:
Shardfall Fit VR is everything I hoped for in a VR fitness game! It’s fast, fun, and had me sweating like crazy by the end of the tutorial. From running in place to punching enemies with hooks and uppercuts, it feels like a full-body workout disguised as a game.

The mechanics, haptics, and challenges are spot on, and it’s super rewarding to progress. If you’re looking for a game that makes fitness exciting, this is it. Shardfall Fit VR might just be my new favorite!

developer_response.body:
HI Kaleah,

"Everything you've hopped for in a VR fitness game", thank you!! This means the world to us. Please stay tuned to future updates coming from the studio. 

We'd love to hear all about your fitness journey and for your feedback on how we can improve the experience. Please join our Discord! https://discord.com/invite/TzCrSpbjAZ

reviews.review_title: It's ok, but has a lot of clunk
reviews.review_description:
Positives:
Looks good and runs well in the headset.  The idea is an interesting one.

Not-so-positives:
The game does not handle real-world rotations.  It doesn't allow you to simply turn in your room.  The game only allows turning (during running segments) with the stick or triggers.  I would much rather be able to just turn in my room and where I'm headed is where I'm looking.  Also, the game force-rotates the user during and after fights, which can be nausea inducing.

Punch hit detection is.... weird.  You don't actually hit the character you're facing off against, you sort of punch in the air in front of it, and it gets counted... or not.  Not infrequently it would just not count punches - I dunno if I was just not punching the right spot in the air (since there is no visible target to hit).  Then, sometimes, it would double-count punches - I think it was happening after I threw a punch then was moving my hand back thru the invisible collider and it would trigger again.  Punching something in the air in order to trigger hit reacts on the characters is fine, but don't make it an invisible target.

There are a lot of points where the game just kind of stops what it's doing and starts up a new state - going from running to an encounter, going from the encounter to getting your rewards - it doesn't feel very organic or flowy.

The game loop is set up less like an adventure game, and more like subway surfers with interstitials.  This half focus on exercise and half focus on game doesn't really work, and kind of takes away from the fun.  I think there probably is a good way to make this work, but the way it's set up right now doesn't really.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to leave a constructive review of Shardfall: FitQuest VR. The whole studio is hugely appreciative of this feedback as we're actively working on updates and patches to improve our game. 

I want to address a few of your points in particular: 

1. Traversal turning. Our team is currently investigating head-based tracking during traversal. This would be a significant change to our traversal system with potential downsides so we are taking our time with this feature and will update the community in due to time. 

2. Punch detection.  Thank you for this feedback on combat and punch detection. We have some improvements coming in a patch next week so please keep an eye out for the update. We're also working on a larger long-term fix for a future patch that we'll release over the coming weeks. 

3.  In regards to the rest of your feedback (re: Game look/meta game). Thank you for this, we're discussing it internally. We'd love to hear any specific ideas you might have discussed in our Discord (https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM)

reviews.review_description: This is the best fitness game I played on the Quest so far, hands down. What sets it apart from other simillar titles is that it offers a real full-body workout - not just punching in place, but also running, jumping, squatting, etc. Most importantly, it’s an actual game, complete with a story, levels, progression, and a roguelike twist. A must-have for anyone looking to get healthier in 2025!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the amazing review! 🙌 We're beyond happy to hear that Shardfall: FitQuest VR stands out as your favourite fitness game on Quest. We’re all about delivering a full-body workout and a true gaming experience, so it’s awesome to know that resonated with you.

We’d love to hear more from you — join the community and chat with us on Discord: https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM.

reviews.review_title: Has potential but not there yet
reviews.review_description:
This game makes me both very happy and very sad at the same time, happy because of how great the idea is, and sad because of how it has many little issues it has that hurt the VR experience. The game being a port explains these issues. Here are my recommendations for the dev team to get to 5 stars:

1. Play Until you Fall. This is a similar idea but with swords and it is extremely immersive, if Shardfall can get to something similar it would be just as good.

2. Fix the hitbox. It doesn't feel like I'm punching the enemy but rather a static wall in front of the enemy. The setting for changing enemy distance is a lazy fix.

3. Give feedback when the enemy is invincible. There are certain times when the enemy can't be hit (like right when the fight starts) and nothing happens when i punch, there should be a force field that surrounds the enemy when I hit it or similar effect to know that.

4. In the running phase turning should be done with moving head, not with controllers.

5. Have an option to remove uphills and downhills in the running phase, these are terrible in vr since in real life its flat and it makes me dizzy 

6. The jump needs to be synced with real life, the obstacle is so high that jumping feels very disorientating you jump a little but in the game its a huge amount

7. The running phase needs more variety, you should add enemies throwing things, more back to back ducks and jumps, parts where you have to run faster and slower etc.

8. Between consecutive enemies the game turns for you which makes me sick. You should ask the player to turn in place instead.

9. The thorn monster takes way too long to do the attack where you have to duck it ruins the flow of the game. You should have a setting to make the attack much faster, it should be part of the flow like punch, punch, duck, duck, punch

10. Again PLEASE look at "Until you Fall" it fixes all of the above and it's a nice reference point.

If the game can fix all these it will definitely be a 5 star from me

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed feedback and for sharing your passion for Shardfall! We truly appreciate you taking the time to provide such specific suggestions on how we can improve the VR experience.

We've passed your feedback directly to our game designers, and we'll definitely be taking your points into consideration as we continue to develop and refine the game. We're particularly interested in exploring your suggestions around hitboxes, enemy feedback, and refining the running/traversal. 

We also appreciate the recommendation to look at "Until You Fall" as a reference point. We're always looking for ways to improve, and we'll be sure to check it out.

We're committed to making Shardfall the best VR fitness experience it can be, and your feedback is invaluable. We encourage you to join our Discord community to continue the conversation and stay up-to-date on the latest developments: 

https://discord.com/invite/TzCrSpbjAZ

reviews.review_description:
Just the tutorial got me sweating buckets. Love how you have to run on the spot. You can opt not to run, but its a fitness app, why wouldn't you? 
You physically have to jump barriers, crouch to go under. It wears you out just getting to an enemy then you have to fight them. Just great.

developer_response.body:
Hi Malc,

It's energising hearing how good of a workout Shardfall: FitQuest VR has been for you so far. Keep up the great work!

We'd love to hear all about your fitness journey and for your feedback on how we can improve the experience. Please join our Discord! https://discord.com/invite/TzCrSpbjAZ

reviews.review_title: Have all fitness apps this a Monster
reviews.review_description:
I love this it's basically running and boxing mixed with Rougelite style.its got enough gamification to trick you into exercises if you not a exercise fan. Please Don't buy if you can't be bothered to run on spot you can skip this by pressing a button but then you might as well get one of the many boxing games. If you add the runnning you will be sweating buckets. Also I love how they have incorporated real ducking and jump tucks into running experience.i wishI didnt have to unlock hard difficultys I want to get on with it on the max difficulty.
Also would be good if the enemy's didn't give you so long to duck and block then it would rely more on your actual reactions. Hope the enemies get alot harder if they do it will beone of my favorite games . Needs more consecutive ducks and jumping faster in running section. Less time to duck enemy's and the enemy that fires projectiles should be way faster. If you keep working on this game it could be massive success thanks so much for porting this from your standalone system

developer_response.body:
Hi Mike,

Thank you for the taking to the time to post this review. Your positive feedback is exciting, and your constructive feedback on duck timing + enemy difficulty is vital to making our game better. We'll ensure it reaches our game designers so it can be implemented.

We'd love to hear all about your fitness journey and for your feedback on how we can improve the experience. Please join our Discord! https://discord.com/invite/TzCrSpbjAZ

reviews.review_description:
I wanted to like this, it looked really cool from the videos - including from certain YouTubers who had nothing but praise for this game. BUT Shardfall was a big shartfail in my view. Running in place would  be fun... IF the game design didn't make you worry about running into your walls/furnitures etc due to some simply bad design decisions. 

First, there is some weird made-up boundary system in place which is not the Quest system boundary. Because of this, the game makes you extremely disoriented anytime the fake warning grid shows up. Adding to this, in a completely unnatural and unnecessary way, the game has artificial stick turning which adds even more to the sense of disorientation AND adds motion sickness to what should be a comfortable experience.  These two factors combined made me nope out of the whole game just a few minutes into the initial tutorial. 

To the devs: please reconsider the design of the running portions and REMOVE TURNING PATHS. Make me run in just a straight line and rethink how gem collection and avoiding obstacles work. Also, don't add a fake boundary but encourage the user to trust the built-in guardian system. I'll try this again if I hear these game-breaking issues are resolved.

I'd highly recommend this game if you want a fun cardio workout. My only complaint is that the in-game running is much faster than how you're actually moving your body. I realize the sense of speed is added to make it more fun and immersive but even turning running down to low it seems way too fast. If there was an option to slow it down some (and maybe steer by turning your body instead of the controller) it would be perfect. Still, I like it enough to give it 5 stars. My average heart rate during my last session was 125 with a peak of 145. So overall a good cardio workout.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the awesome review and for the 5-star rating! We're thrilled that you're enjoying Shardfall as a fun way to get your cardio in and that you're seeing great results with your heart rate.

We appreciate your feedback about the running speed.  We understand that the current speed might feel too fast for some players, even on the low setting.  We're exploring ways to provide more fine-tuned control over the running speed in future updates.

Your suggestion about steering with body turning is a great one! We're already looking into alternative control schemes to make the running experience more intuitive and immersive.

We're committed to making Shardfall the best VR fitness game it can be, and your feedback is invaluable in that process.  We encourage you to join our Discord community to share your thoughts and connect with other players: 

https://discord.com/invite/TzCrSpbjAZ

reviews.review_description: It’s a great game and workout, but you should add Mixed Reality support. When running, players tend to move from their original position, which can lead to collisions with objects.

reviews.review_title: Much more fun than the gym!
reviews.review_description: I was looking for a perfect merge between gaming and fitness and I think this game got it just right, I thought it was gonna be easy but it actually is an intense workout! After half an hour I was sweating! I prefer the fighting bits but there's an option to autorun if you're not a fan of jumping. Definitely a fun workout solution to stay fit!

I absolutely love the game. I love its immersive and cool design, gameplay is amazing and it is so fun I can't stop playing help me.

There are quite a few people saying about motion sickness, they are either inexperienced in vr or they just haven't gone to the setting where you can turn all sorts of things to combat it on.

People also say that their punches don't register, I have not experienced this problem personally but again you can go into settings and set the punch requirements to low so that it is easier to punch.

One of my favourites, I will cherish it forever.

Update: had to decrease because punches don't register I see what they mean

reviews.review_title: I wanted to love this game      but
reviews.review_description: the physics dont work      if you throw a specific punch at a specific time your ok  but not otherwise     as in   your expected to throw only a specific punch at a specific time or you get no reaction      its like dragons lair     from the dawn of arcade consoles      you make the specific right choice at the specific right time or you get no reaction ((in this game )  of course in dragons lair wrong choices could be fatal)       so this is worse than an ancient arcade game        this is not boxing    its paint by numbers    and an extremely unsatifying experience if your expecting a boxing game    a boxing game should have an opponent you could attack in more than one way    that would respond appropriately   this game    you are ecpected to respond in an extremly limited way and so will your opponent

This app is unreal! I bought it before the free trial was finished.

Best fitness app in my opinion as you are actually moving your lower legs when you run in place. Other apps do squats, but that's about the most lower body activity you get.

Great for releasing stress as well with the fighting! 

So much fun. Amazing job, team 💜

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the fantastic review! 💜 We're so happy to hear you're loving the game and getting a full-body workout, especially with the lower body movement. It's awesome that the fighting and running are helping you relieve stress while having fun!

We really appreciate the kind words — keep enjoying those workouts, and feel free to join us on Discord to share more thoughts with the team: https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM.

reviews.review_description: This will definitely get your heart rate up! Just the tutorial was a lot of fun. It does remind me of RingFit (which I love). I really like being able to compare my results (calories burned/distance, etc) with my Apple Watch so I can make sure I’m getting the most out of my work out. It makes working out a lot of fun and I’m looking forward to playing agin later 😄

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

Glad to hear Shardfall: FitQuest VR did a good job getting your heart going! We're thrilled with the quality of exercise our game is giving the community. 

We'd also love to hear all about your fitness journey and for your feedback on how we can improve the experience. Please join our Discord! https://discord.com/invite/TzCrSpbjAZ

reviews.review_title: Great 4 Heart Rate, Bad 4 Blood Pressure
reviews.review_description:
This is a great workout, the best I've found so far, but it is SOOO aggravating. 

The run detection is intermittent at best, the steering is nauseating (just let me rotate in place, that shouldn't be difficult) and the combat feedback is almost nonexistent. 

Lots and lots of immersion-breaking modal popups to tell you stuff, but very little of it is useful. 

Sometimes my punch hits, sometimes the same punch doesn't. Why?  No clue.  Sometimes I win, sometimes I get knocked out   Why? No idea. Looked like the same animation to me. 

And Oh, Boy, do they want to sell you resistance bands!  How about you get the game to work, first.  

Great concept though.  Hope they get the interface worked out before I get sick of it.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic but could be slightly better
reviews.review_description:
This is exactly what I look for in VR...trick me into exercising. I haven't played long, but I have notes. 

My favorite thing about it is moving by actually running. I literally just bought the Kat Loco S system that lets you do this in some other games, and here it is natively! Very much wish more games would adopt this. That being said, I tried it on my rebounder instead of jogging in place and felt even more of a workout because the rebounder forces you to keep your core engaged to keep balance. Still works in the game!

It's highly customizable. As someone else said, you can tweak punch settings, etc. I like the quick play that lets you choose length, style, and difficulty level for your workout. I like you can jump or duck with buttons if you aren't able physically. I have comfort settings turned off but I could see people getting ill easily while running. Maybe we could go not quite so fast but shorten the distance? 

Wish there was a greater variety of opponents (but I have barely gotten started). I'm starting to hate the flower that throws bombs. Just can't get the timing right, I guess. The robots feel like I'm in the old rock 'em, sock 'em game from last century (dear god). But the punching does get me sweating, for sure. 

A greater variety of environments and music would be welcome too, but I don't know how hard that would be to implement. 

Otherwise you have a solid, entertaining workout game here!

Update: Sorry to subtract a star but I'm beyond frustrated. Can we have a hint on the big plants throwing berries? I'm expending all my energy trying to punch "unblockables" (which I can't tell they're unblockable until I notice the tiny screen to my right, after I've punched). Can't dodge them. I had to quit my run from exhaustion and frustration. Can we have a way to omit them from the workout? Seriously. Eff them.
It's okay but hate the way that it doesn't feel like you are punching the enemies, always seem to be out of reach

This is not a bad game but...
While running turning with head is a must.
Punches do not feel that good. Enemy connection missing.
Add male protagonist.
While above 3 will not get fixed it is a refund.

Can't even get past the tutorial as the punches won't register. Since there's no feedback on whether it's too fast or too slow or whether I'm punching in the correct spot it's impossible to improve. Currently waiting for Meta to approve a refund.

developer_response.body:
We're really sorry to hear you're having trouble with the tutorial and punch registration! We understand how frustrating that can be.

The good news is that we've just released an update today that specifically addresses gesture recognition and provides a smoother onboarding experience:

(1) Improved Gesture Recognition: This update includes significant improvements to how punches are detected, so hopefully, this resolves the issues you were facing.
(2) Enhanced Tutorial: We've also revamped the tutorial to provide clearer instructions and guidance, which should make it easier to learn the game mechanics.
We encourage you to give the updated tutorial another try and see if it resolves the problems.

If you're still having trouble or have further feedback, we'd love to hear from you on our Discord: https://discord.com/invite/TzCrSpbjAZ

We appreciate your patience and understanding as we continue to improve Shardfall!

reviews.review_description: Tiring, which is great. I thought the tutorial was quite tough... and then I started playing the first level! Enemies look great, and you get a really solid workout in.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your review! We're glad to hear you're getting a great workout and enjoying Shardfall's enemies. The tutorial is definitely a great workout, but it sounds like you're crushing it already. Keep it up — there’s lots more adventure ahead!

You can also chat about the game and with the team directly on our Discord (https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM).

reviews.review_description: It feels like playing an actual video game, and not just doing random fitness exercises to defeat enemies (looking at you Ring Fit lol). The movements make sense (you do a chest press to throw a spell, or duck only when you need to avoid an attack), and it made me lose a bunch of calories while having fun, which is the main thing I look for in a game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review! We're thrilled to hear the gameplay feels natural and purposeful — we’ve worked hard to make the movements immersive and intuitive. And burning calories while having fun? That’s exactly what we aim for!

Feel free to share more thoughts or chat with the team on our Discord: https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM.

reviews.review_title: Sooo good! I've found my thing
reviews.review_description: Nothing else to say, just super tough workouts mixed with dope music and a really cool fantasy setting.  love this game

reviews.review_title: Perfect for a potato like myself!
reviews.review_description: My friends know me as mash potato because I never want to leave the couch 😅😅 this game broke the spell and I'm finally moving again! Super fun and well designed, incredibly engaging. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the fantastic review! We’re so glad to hear Shardfall broke the couch spell and got you moving! It’s awesome to know you’re finding it fun, engaging, and well-designed. Keep it up — you’re doing amazing!

We’d love to hear more from you on Discord, so feel free to join the conversation: https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM.

reviews.review_description: Shardfall is an absolute knockout—literally! The boxing action in this game hits harder than a freight train, and the intensity keeps your adrenaline maxed out. The mechanics are so clean, it feels like the punches are flying straight out of your fists. The visuals? Chef’s kiss. The sound design? Every jab, hook, and uppercut lands with a satisfying thud that’ll make you feel like a total beast. If you're not sweating by the end of a match, you're not playing it right. This is the VR boxing experience we've been waiting for. Wow!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the incredible review! We’re beyond excited to hear how much you’re enjoying Shardfall’s boxing action. Knowing the mechanics, visuals, and sound design are hitting the mark (literally!) means the world to us. And you’re right—if you’re not sweating, you’re not going hard enough!

We’d love for you to join the conversation on Discord and share more with the community: https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM. Keep crushing it!

reviews.review_title: Fun and engaging way to work out!
reviews.review_description:
I love doing squat workouts irl but finding the motivation can be hard. When fighting you sometimes have to squat lots of times- I ended up doing so many squats without realising because I got really into the fight.

I like the combination of fighting and running because I think I get bored doing too much of one type of fitness for a long time.

Seeing my stats tracked across sessions is great. I’d love to customise my character more :)

developer_response.body:
It’s great to hear that the game’s balance of fighting and running is keeping things exciting, and that you’re getting in those sneaky squats while having fun! We’re also glad the stat tracking is helping you stay motivated.

We’re always looking for ways to improve, so we’ll keep that character customization in mind for future updates. Don’t forget to join us on Discord for more updates and to chat with the team: https://discord.com/invite/NPXnSFM.
